main.description: Multiplexed real-time sync. A single WebSocket connection carries `#messageDiff` (applied directly to the client cache) and `#invalidate` (triggers an HTTP re-fetch of the named query) frames for all topics the connection has subscribed to. The client opens the WS with a `?ticket=<...>` query parameter obtained via `space.roomy.auth.getConnectionTicket`. Client-to-server messages are JSON text frames (`sub` / `unsub` / `cursor`) — see the appserver XRPC spec; lexicon subscriptions only describe server-to-client frames.

messageDiff.description: Add/update/remove operations for messages in a subscribed room. Applied directly to the client cache without an HTTP round-trip.

seq.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number used for cursor-based replay on reconnect.

message.description: Present for add/update; absent for remove.

invalidate.description: Signals that a query's data is stale. The client maps this to `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [nsid, params] })`.

params.description: Param map (string keys, string values) reproducing the query key parameters.

error.description: Terminal error. The server closes the connection after sending this frame.
